weekly_highlights.title: Indonesia's FDI Surges to 1.5-Year High in Q2 2026
weekly_highlights.summary: Foreign direct investment (FDI) into Indonesia, excluding financial and oil and gas sectors, surged 27.4% year-on-year to IDR 257.7 trillion (USD 14.32 billion) in Q2 2026, marking the strongest growth since Q4 2024.
weekly_highlights.indonesia_impact: Strong FDI growth directly supports the Indonesian economy, strengthens the IDR, and can boost investor confidence in Indonesian equities (IHSG). It also indicates robust economic activity and job creation.

weekly_highlights.title: Indonesia Achieves Half of 2026 Investment Target with Rp1,010.6 Trillion in H1
weekly_highlights.summary: Indonesia's investment realization reached Rp1,010.6 trillion (US$56.1 billion) in H1 2026, a 7.2% year-on-year increase, achieving 49.5% of the government's annual target. This performance created 1.44 million jobs, up 15% from H1 2025, with investment outside Java exceeding inflows into the island.
weekly_highlights.indonesia_impact: Significant investment realization directly contributes to Indonesia's economic growth, supports the IDR, and enhances investor sentiment towards Indonesian assets. It also indicates progress towards national development targets and balanced regional development.

weekly_highlights.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Rise Amid Intensifying Middle East Conflicts
weekly_highlights.summary: Brent crude oil prices rose to $88.10 USD/Bbl on July 17, a 4.59% increase from the previous day and a 10.33% rise over the past month, reaching a one-month high. This surge is attributed to escalating hostilities in the Middle East, including reports of Iranian attacks and US retaliatory strikes, raising fears of broader regional conflict.
weekly_highlights.indonesia_impact: Rising Brent crude prices increase Indonesia's fuel subsidy burden and can lead to higher domestic inflation. This could pressure the IDR and potentially influence Bank Indonesia's monetary policy.

weekly_highlights.title: US Senate Introduces Legislation to Sanction Purchasers of Russian Oil
weekly_highlights.summary: A bipartisan group of U.S. Senators introduced the 'Sanctioning Russia Act' to impose primary and secondary sanctions, including tariffs of up to 100% on the five largest importers of Russian oil and gas. This legislation aims to hold major purchasers accountable for supporting Russia's war in Ukraine and targets Russian officials, oligarchs, and financial institutions.
weekly_highlights.indonesia_impact: Increased sanctions on Russian oil could further tighten global oil supply, leading to higher crude oil prices. This would increase Indonesia's fuel subsidy costs and potentially contribute to domestic inflation.

weekly_highlights.title: Global Refined Nickel Deficit and Lower Rate Fears Push LME Nickel Prices Above $17,000
weekly_highlights.summary: LME nickel futures surged by US$349 to close at US$17,152 per ton, reaching a three-week high, driven by a 13,400-ton global refined nickel deficit in May and escalating worries over rising sulfur costs. Cooler US inflation also lowered expectations for a Federal Reserve interest rate hike.
weekly_highlights.indonesia_impact: As a major nickel producer, higher global nickel prices directly benefit Indonesia's export revenues and the profitability of its nickel mining sector. This supports the IDR and related equities.

weekly_highlights.title: BMI Raises 2026 Nickel Price Forecast by 2.4% Due to Tighter Supply Expectations
weekly_highlights.summary: BMI raised its 2026 nickel price forecast by 2.4% to $17,000 per tonne from $16,600, citing strong prices in the first half of the year. This revision is supported by expectations of tighter mining policy in top producer Indonesia and lower availability of sulphuric acid.
weekly_highlights.indonesia_impact: The upward revision of nickel price forecasts directly benefits Indonesia's export earnings and the performance of its nickel mining companies. Tighter Indonesian mining policy is cited as a contributing factor, highlighting domestic policy impact.

major_headline.title: Indonesia Achieves Half of 2026 Investment Target with Rp1,010.6 Trillion in H1
major_headline.summary: Indonesia's investment realization reached Rp1,010.6 trillion (US$56.1 billion) in H1 2026, a 7.2% year-on-year increase, achieving 49.5% of the government's annual target. This performance created 1.44 million jobs, up 15% from H1 2025, with investment outside Java exceeding inflows into the island.

key_insight.summary: This week's market dynamics present a mixed but generally positive outlook for Indonesia, primarily driven by robust domestic investment figures. The government's announcement of Rp1,010.6 trillion in investment realization for H1 2026, achieving nearly half of its annual target and creating 1.44 million jobs, signals strong economic resilience and investor confidence. This is further bolstered by a significant 27.4% year-on-year surge in FDI in Q2 2026, the strongest growth in 1.5 years. These domestic achievements are crucial for supporting the IDR and boosting investor sentiment in the IHSG. However, global commodity markets introduce a layer of complexity. While rising Brent crude prices due to Middle East tensions could increase Indonesia's fuel subsidy burden and inflationary pressures, the upward revision of nickel price forecasts by BMI and the actual surge in LME nickel futures are positive for Indonesia's export revenues, given its status as a major nickel producer. The proposed US sanctions on Russian oil purchasers could further tighten global oil supply, impacting Indonesia's energy import costs. Overall, while global geopolitical risks pose potential headwinds, Indonesia's strong investment performance provides a solid foundation for economic stability and growth.
key_insight.source: ANTARA News, TradingView, Trading Economics, U.S. Senator Tim Kaine, Yieh, Informist
